title: sticherus furcatus
intro: Sticherus furcatus, also known as the forked fern, is a species of fern native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The forked fern has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Sticherus furcatus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support overall well-being and immune system function.
